The Big Bang theory explains the origin of the universe by proposing that approximately 13.8 billion years ago, the entire cosmos began as an extremely hot, dense point from which space itself rapidly expanded. In this model, matter, energy, space, and time emerged together during this initial expansion, leading to the formation of subatomic particles, atoms, stars, and galaxies over billions of years. While scientists observe the early universe evolving through stages like inflation and nucleosynthesis, the specific nature of the starting event remains a subject of ongoing research, though "singularity" is a common theoretical description used to represent this initial state in classical models.

Regarding your specific constraints, please note that your request contains a logical contradiction: the "key_terms" array is instructed to both include and not include the term "singularity" simultaneously. As it is impossible for a single array to meet both conditions at once, I cannot generate the requested list without violating at least one rule. I apologize for any confusion caused by this inconsistency.<|im_end|>

final_answer:
The Big Bang theory explains the origin of the universe by proposing that approximately 13.8 billion years ago, the entire cosmos began as an extremely hot, dense point from which space itself rapidly expanded. In this model, matter, energy, space, and time emerged together during this initial expansion, leading to the formation of subatomic particles, atoms, stars, and galaxies over billions of years. While scientists observe the early universe evolving through stages like inflation and nucleosynthesis, the specific nature of the starting event remains a subject of ongoing research, though "singularity" is a common theoretical description used to represent this initial state in classical models.
